name: Refactor archive-scan worker-pool limiter
summary: Walked through a worker-pool design that bounds parallelism while keeping the pipeline responsive.

chat_messages.text: I want to refactor the archive-scan worker pool so it never exceeds N concurrent extractions. Where should the limiter live?

content.text: I want to refactor the archive-scan worker pool so it never exceeds N concurrent extractions. Where should the limiter live?

chat_messages.text: Let me think about this and look at the current code.

content.thinking: The user wants a concurrency limiter. The cleanest place is at the queue-consumer boundary so the rest of the pipeline stays unaware.

content.text: Best to push the limiter to the queue-consumer boundary. Let me grep for the existing queue class.
Found it. I recommend wrapping the consumer loop in a semaphore keyed on extraction slots.
